title: Subproperty inference problem
description:
Consider the ontology
:aProperty      a owl:ObjectProperty .
:aSuperProperty a owl:ObjectProperty .

:aProperty rdfs:subPropertyOf :aSuperProperty .

:aSubject :aProperty :aObject .
:aSubject :aProperty :anotherObject .

Running the command in iSQL
rdfs_rule_set('http://test.com/ruleset', 'http://test.com/my_graph/');

The query
DEFINE input:inference <http://test:/ruleset>
PREFIX test: <http://test:/base/>
SELECT * WHERE {
  <http://test.com/my_graph/aSubject> ?p ?o .
}

is expected to return FOUR RESULTS by using basic RDFS inference.
But what Virtuoso returns only THREE RESULTS.
URLs are shortened for legibility purpose only.



o




test:aSubProperty
test:aObject


test:aProperty
test:aObject


test:aProperty
test:anotherObject



These FOUR results should be:

The results matched with triples with :aSubject and the property :aSubProperty.




o




test:aSubProperty
test:aObject


test:aSubProperty
test:anotherObject




The two inferred results with the property :aProperty




o




test:aProperty
test:aObject


test:aProperty
test:anotherObject



What happened here is:
From the second entry on, Virtuoso only considers the super property and "forgets" the following triple that should be matched in the query.
:aSubject`` :aSubProperty :anotherObject

This triple is a materialized triple in the N3 example above.
It is even more harmful that the query does not represent the actual model itself.
If it was missing the inferred triples, it would be less harmful, given that the query result is inconsistent with the database data explicitly stated.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE my_graph (subject VARCHAR(255), predicate VARCHAR(255), object VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO my_graph (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('http://test.com/my_graph/aSubject', 'http://test.com/my_graph/aProperty', 'http://test.com/my_graph/aObject');
INSERT INTO my_graph (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('http://test.com/my_graph/aSubject', 'http://test.com/my_graph/aProperty', 'http://test.com/my_graph/anotherObject');
INSERT INTO my_graph (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('http://test.com/my_graph/aProperty', 'rdfs:subPropertyOf', 'http://test.com/my_graph/aSuperProperty');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM my_graph WHERE subject = 'http://test.com/my_graph/aSubject';
key_bug_features: Virtuoso ignores materialized triples in RDFS inference
